steps.title: Match slopes with different intercepts
steps.content: For no solution, the second equation needs the same slope but a different constant. Choice B: $\frac{1}{3}x=4y$ scales to $x=12y$, same slope, different constant.

steps.title: Identify the choice
steps.content: Graph the original equation with each possible second equation. A no-solution system appears as two distinct parallel lines. Only choice B, $\frac{1}{3}x=4y$, has the same slope as the original line but a different intercept. That matches choice B — B is correct.
